figures.caption: Table 1: Comparison of detection performance on PKU-DAVIS-SOD and DSEC datasets. Runtime is measured in milliseconds for a batch size of 1 on an NVIDIA Tesla V100 GPU.

Figure 2: Overview of the proposed Hyper-FEOD framework. Hyper-FEOD takes event streams and RGB frames as input, and employs a two-stream encoder to extract multi-modal features. S-HCF leverages event-guided sparsity to perform high-order hypergraph interaction on motion-critical tokens, enabling efficient non-local cross-modal modeling and superior multi-modal fusion. FG-MoE utilizes regionspecialized hypergraph experts and pixel-level gating to adaptively refine boundary, texture and background features, achieving stable training and precise end-to-end feature enhancement.

Figure 3: Overview of the proposed SHCF module. HIFI uses CHC to generate cross-modal hyperedges and AHA to simultaneously perform intra- and inter-modality high-order feature interaction in a aggregation-and-distribution manner.
